requirements.title: Create data-driven Scenario Model and first unclear-requirements scenario fixture.
requirements.acceptance: Scenario data expresses nodes, edges, steps, choices, terminal options, and source refs outside JSX.

requirements.title: Implement active scenario slice and stepwise command-choice interaction.
requirements.acceptance: Users can see current command, output conditions, next choices, and active path without executing commands.

requirements.title: Add continuation routes, source badges, and local validation checklist.
requirements.acceptance: Terminal choices, cited/pending source status, and local comprehension checks appear in the detail panel.

constraints.constraint: Scenario data must live outside React rendering components.
Phase 1 should not introduce backend, login, LLM service, or real status.json parser.
Alternatives, terminal routes, and source badges belong in the detail panel.
Citation policy prefers command definitions, then guide docs, then Codex skill mirrors.
Validation checklist pass threshold is implementation discretion.

domain.problem_statement: Users need to choose Maestro commands step by step from scenario state.
domain.audience: Internal validators and future Maestro users learning command sequences.

non_goals.title: Full visual design system polish
non_goals.rationale: Deferred until core scenario loop works.

non_goals.title: Graph library adoption
non_goals.rationale: Deferred until manual SVG layout becomes a blocker.

non_goals.title: LLM-backed recommendation or real status parsing

insights.summary: The implementation gap is missing scenario semantics, not graph rendering capability.
Command definitions should be the primary citation source for scenario command facts.

open_questions.question: Should checklist completion be informational or required for MVP acceptance?
